violations.text: Crates of vegetables were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC had staff remove from floor. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025

The handle to the walk-in is in poor repair, observed with latch missing. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. PIC stated part is on order and scheduled for delivery today. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025

Raw animal foods (chicken, eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (produce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC informed Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Sink bay was drained and refilled to proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025

Chicken was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Melon on ice observed 40-47 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed chicken; ice added to cold holding. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff, ground beef was placed hot in the refrigerator in a covered plastic container. Container was uncovered to facilitate cooling. PIC discussed with staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025
Crates of produce were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in and kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025
Wooden spoons were not in good repair, observed with chips/cracks. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
Ventilation hoods in kitchen and service area observed with an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

Handle of ice scoop was observed stored in the ice. Utensils used to dispense ice must be with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024
Single service items were observed stored on the floor in the front kitchen area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the prep area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

Raw animal foods (such as fish, shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cabbage, lemons and dressings) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

Beans in the Hot Hold Unit was observed held at 129 and 131 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed beans. Unit was adjusted to a higher temperature. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

Potato Salad, Chicken Salad, Salsa, Pineapple, and Caesar Dressing, Salad Greens were observed held at 48-62 degrees Fahrenheit at the service line cold station. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were prepared recently and removed to other refrigeration to blast chill. Cold Holding unit was observed filled with water with an ambient temperature of 46 degrees. Water was removed to allow unit to properly chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

Frozen pork was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the sugar cane juicer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023
Kitchen Hoods were observed with an accumulation of dust. Physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/25/2023
